# HERD

> HERD is an AI-native B2B platform for marketing networks.
> Currently 45 documented entries across 5 layers (Networks, Solutions, Tools, Blocks, Integrations) plus a meta namespace.

## Documentation

- [Handbook home](https://herd.app/admin/handbook): Overview of the entire system, organized by layer.

## Layers

- [Networks](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/networks): Layer that organizes the different types of networks HERD supports: corporate, market, and multi-market.
- [Solutions](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/solutions): Layer that organizes feature packages focused on business value: by market, by department, by segment.
- [Tools](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/tools): Layer that organizes tools by functional nature: financial, legal, marketing, sales.
- [Blocks](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/blocks): Layer that organizes data blocks by nature: identity, media, time, commercial, financial.
- [Integrations](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/integrations): Layer that organizes integrations with external systems by purpose: payment, calendar, communication.
- [Areas](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/areas): Structural layer of product macro-divisions. Each area groups tools by their function in the product.
- [Refactor](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/refactor): Pre-1.5.6f structural refactor stages aligning code with the canonical architecture.

## Categories

- [Corporate Network](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/networks/corporate-network): A company's internal network: organization, channels, profiles, departments, permissions.
- [Market Network](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/networks/market-network): The external network connecting multiple companies in the same market.
- [Financial](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/tools/financial): Tools that organize, process, and audit financial flows of the product, from internal accounting to integration with payment and billing systems.
- [Infrastructure](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/tools/infrastructure): Cross-cutting functional tools that sustain other features without belonging to a specific business domain — events, queues, observability.
- [Financial](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/blocks/financial): Financial data primitives — records, flows, immutable events — that serve as the foundation for tools and product flows.
- [Miscellaneous](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/blocks/miscellaneous): Blocks not yet classified into a specific category. Temporary accommodation — debt to resolve during backfill.
- [Communication](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/areas/communication): Communication between people in the system. Tools that enable conversation, messaging, and human-human or human-agent interaction.
- [Identity](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/areas/identity): Identity and knowledge area. Tools that manage who is in the system (people, organizations) and what the system knows.
- [Infrastructure](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/areas/infrastructure): Under-the-hood platform infrastructure. Tools that document, configure, or operate the platform itself — invisible in normal flow.
- [Notification](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/areas/notification): Notification center. Centralizes alerts, reminders, and event-driven communications across the platform.
- [R0 — Manifest Registry Foundation](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/refactor/r0-manifest-registry): Refactor foundation: pre-refactor cleanup, architectural content reform in the Handbook, and introduction of the kind discriminator in the manifest registry.
- [R1.5 — Re-investigation R3-R8 Reclassifications](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/refactor/r1-5-reclassifications-revision): Doc-first revision of R3-R8 scope based on factual investigation of code state during R1.
- [R1 — Tools Foundation Reconciliation](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/refactor/r1-tools-foundation): Reconciliation of the existing tools infrastructure with the discriminator schema established in R0.2. Categories as 5th technical category.
- [R2.5 — Network Split (Organization + Directory)](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/refactor/r2-5-network-split): Split of the current Network top-level into two distinct top-level features: Organization (institutional structure) and Directory (people structure). Includes channel disambiguation.
- [R2 — Areas Foundation](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/refactor/r2-areas-foundation): Areas layer — product macro-divisions where tools are positioned. 5th canonical technical category replacing top-level-feature.
- [R3 — Packages Refinement](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/refactor/r3-packages-refinement): Refinement of the packages tool in sales — investigate whether 'products bundled' concept warrants a block-group of products inside the tool.
- [R4 — Campaigns Convergence (block → tool)](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/refactor/r4-campaigns-convergence): Promote campaigns from active block to tool in marketing. Converge paths and delete existing coming-soon placeholder.
- [R5 — Subscriptions Split + Offering Creation](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/refactor/r5-subscriptions-split): Split subscriptions monolith into residual block (customer's real subscription) + subscription-offering tool (sellable catalog). Consolidate divergent paths.
- [R6 — Routines as Tool](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/refactor/r6-routines-tool): Promote routines from block to tool. Create /admin/routines/ + dedicated sidebar item.
- [R7 — Agents as Tool (consolidate dual surface)](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/refactor/r7-agents-tool): Promote agents from block to tool. Consolidate dual surface (admin/agents already exists alongside admin/blocks/agents).
- [Transaction](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/areas/transaction): Commercial transactions. Customer-facing tools to sell, buy, and exchange value.
- [Workflow](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/areas/workflow): Operational workflow hub. Internal tools to manage day-to-day work — finance ops, sales ops, legal ops, milestones, dashboards.

## Meta

- [Glossary](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/meta/glossary): Global glossary aggregated from terms defined across all Handbook entries.
- [Handbook](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/meta/handbook): HERD's documentation system: architecture, conventions, and operations.
- [llms.txt](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/meta/llms-txt): Public file at /llms.txt following the llmstxt.org convention — guides LLMs about what HERD is, where to find canonical content, and how to access it programmatically.
- [MCP Server](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/meta/mcp): Model Context Protocol server that exposes the Handbook as a navigable resource for AI assistants via stdio (local clients) and HTTP (remote clients).

## Programmatic access

The Handbook is also available via Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. See [MCP Server](https://herd.app/admin/handbook/meta/mcp) for stdio and HTTP transport details.
